Rive Runtime Wrapper Completion\r\n\r\nUpdate the runtime wrapper and contract system.\r\n\r\n## Required runtime behavior\r\n\r\nSupport:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nloading\r\nready\r\nfallback\r\nfailed\r\ntimed-out\r\npaused\r\nhidden\r\nunmounted\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Load timeout\r\n\r\nA Rive runtime may not remain â€œloadingâ€ indefinitely.\r\n\r\nUse an asset-specific bounded timeout.\r\n\r\nRecord:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nasset ID\r\nstart time\r\nready time\r\ntimeout\r\nfailure reason\r\nfallback selected\r\n```\r\n\r\n## State-signal queue\r\n\r\nSignals arriving before readiness must follow a documented policy:\r\n\r\n* retain latest authoritative state;\r\n* discard obsolete transient hover signals;\r\n* preserve required triggers where safe;\r\n* apply final authoritative state immediately after readiness.\r\n\r\nDo not replay stale validation or hover events after delayed load.\r\n\r\n## Visibility\r\n\r\nWhen:\r\n\r\n* document is hidden;\r\n* component is offscreen;\r\n* route is inactive;\r\n\r\npause unnecessary internal motion.\r\n\r\nDo not lose semantic state.\r\n\r\n## Cleanup\r\n\r\nOn unmount:\r\n\r\n* release Rive instance;\r\n* release event listeners;\r\n* release timers;\r\n* clear queued transient signals;\r\n* preserve no detached canvas or WebGL context;\r\n* return runtime metrics to baseline.\r\n\r\n## Context loss\r\n\r\nHandle WebGL/context failure with fallback.\r\n\r\nDo not render a blank object.\r\n\r\n## Rapid state replacement\r\n\r\nTest:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nvalidating -> rejected\r\nvalidating -> accepted -> opening\r\nteased -> partial -> ready\r\nopening -> route unmount\r\nwaiting -> launched -> opening\r\n```\r\n\r\nOnly authoritative final state may remain.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 16. Rive Accessibility\r\n\r\nEach Rive object is decorative or state-illustrative.\r\n\r\nIt must not be the sole semantic source.\r\n\r\nRequired:\r\n\r\n* meaningful state text outside the canvas;\r\n* Rive canvas hidden from assistive technology unless a supported accessible wrapper is deliberately used;\r\n* focus never enters internal decorative vectors;\r\n* pointer behavior has keyboard equivalent;\r\n* color is not the only state signal;\r\n* motion is not the only state signal;\r\n* reduced motion reaches the same final meaning;\r\n* fallback has the same accessible label and state description.\r\n\r\nRequired state announcements include:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nInvitation accepted\r\nInvitation rejected\r\nInvitation expired\r\nInvitation revoked\r\nJournal ready to open\r\nVoyage course set\r\nConnection lost\r\nFinale ready\r\nFinale unlocked\r\n```\r\n\r\nUse concise, non-spammy live-region behavior.\r\n\r\nDo not announce every animation frame or input change.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 17. Lottie Runtime Completion\r\n\r\nLottie remains a contained-effect runtime.\r\n\r\nIt must not become an application state machine.\r\n\r\n## Existing assets\r\n\r\nAt minimum:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nmoonlitWaves\r\nrollingFog\r\ninkBloom\r\n```\r\n\r\nAlso identify every added Lottie asset introduced by Phase 3 or Phase 4.\r\n\r\nReconcile each with the governing matrix.\r\n\r\n## Required controller API\r\n\r\nImplement or confirm a stable controller such as:\r\n\r\n```ts\r\ntype LottieEffectHandle = {\r\n  play(): void;\r\n  pause(): void;\r\n  stop(): void;\r\n  setSpeed(speed: number): void;\r\n  goToFrame(frame: number): void;\r\n  playSegment(\r\n    segment: [number, number],\r\n    options?: {\r\n      force?: boolean;\r\n      onComplete?: () => void;\r\n    }\r\n  ): void;\r\n  setMotionPolicy(policy: ResolvedMotionPolicy): void;\r\n  destroy(): void;\r\n};\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe exact API may differ, but it must support in-place policy updates.\r\n\r\n## Mode changes\r\n\r\nChanging:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nfull -> gentle\r\ngentle -> full\r\nfull -> reduced\r\nreduced -> full\r\nbrowser preference after mount\r\n```\r\n\r\nmust not automatically destroy and reload the JSON unless a renderer-level configuration truly requires it.\r\n\r\nAmbient loops should:\r\n\r\n* maintain logical continuity;\r\n* update speed in place;\r\n* pause or move to a representative frame in reduced mode.\r\n\r\nNon-looping effects must not replay solely because mode changed.\r\n\r\n## Visibility\r\n\r\nPause ambient Lottie effects when:\r\n\r\n* document is hidden;\r\n* intersection policy says offscreen;\r\n* route/component is inactive.\r\n\r\nResume without resetting semantic state unless the contract specifies restart.\r\n\r\n## Load timeout\r\n\r\nAdd:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nready timeout\r\nmalformed JSON handling\r\nrenderer error handling\r\nnetwork/import failure handling\r\nbounded retry\r\nfallback\r\n```\r\n\r\nNo Lottie container may remain blank indefinitely.\r\n\r\n## Representative frame\r\n\r\nEach Lottie contract must define an intentional reduced/static frame.\r\n\r\nDo not blindly use frame zero.\r\n\r\nFor example:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nmoonlitWaves -> stable moonlit water frame\r\nrollingFog -> restrained readable fog frame\r\ninkBloom -> completed ink frame\r\n```\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 18. Lottie: Moonlit Waves\r\n\r\nRequired behavior:\r\n\r\n* load once per mounted asset instance;\r\n* loop only while visible and permitted;\r\n* gentle mode reduces speed/amplitude where the animation supports it;\r\n* reduced mode uses representative still;\r\n* route or role transition does not restart it without a declared reason;\r\n* asset-ready gateway reveal remains stable;\r\n* no blank-to-animation flash;\r\n* no duplicate wave runtimes on one surface unless explicitly designed.\r\n\r\nValidate on:\r\n\r\n* landing;\r\n* legacy access if retained;\r\n* any waiting-room ambience assigned by the matrix.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 19. Lottie: Rolling Fog\r\n\r\nRequired behavior:\r\n\r\n* load once per mounted asset instance;\r\n* pause while hidden;\r\n* avoid several simultaneous fog runtimes where one shared layer is sufficient;\r\n* avoid mode-change recreation;\r\n* preserve opacity/containment ownership;\r\n* never cover required controls;\r\n* never become a substitute for authoritative map fog state.\r\n\r\nDifferentiate:\r\n\r\n```text\r\ndecorative atmospheric fog\r\nauthoritative chart reveal mask\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe first may use Lottie.\r\n\r\nThe second remains GSAP/SVG/CSS state controlled by authoritative map data.\r\n\r\nDo not make Lottie decide which map territory is revealed.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 20. Lottie: Ink Bloom\r\n\r\n`inkBloom` must no longer autoplay on mount.\r\n\r\n## Required semantic control\r\n\r\nThe effect must begin only at an approved semantic label such as:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nink-heading\r\nink-story\r\nink-objective\r\nink-riddle\r\nannotation-ink\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe exact label depends on the scene contract.\r\n\r\n## Required behavior\r\n\r\n* preload or become ready before the intended label where feasible;\r\n* remain hidden or at initial frame until commanded;\r\n* play the intended segment;\r\n* report completion;\r\n* never replay because motion mode changed;\r\n* never replay because visibility returned;\r\n* never start before required text target preflight;\r\n* reduced mode shows completed representative frame;\r\n* failure leaves readable text.\r\n\r\n## Multiple ink effects\r\n\r\nIf multiple ink moments exist:\r\n\r\n* use named segments;\r\n* do not mount multiple duplicate Lottie players unnecessarily;\r\n* ensure each segment corresponds to actual visible content;\r\n* do not overwrite text rendering or delay readability beyond the scene budget.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 21. Additional Lottie Opportunity Sweep\r\n\r\nReview every accepted Codex and OA animation assigned to Phase 5.\r\n\r\nFor each, determine whether Lottie is actually appropriate.\r\n\r\nGood Lottie candidates:\r\n\r\n* contained ambient waves;\r\n* contained fog illustration;\r\n* ink bloom;\r\n* small illustrated dust or particles;\r\n* self-contained decorative reveal.\r\n\r\nBad Lottie candidates:\r\n\r\n* form state;\r\n* navigation;\r\n* route transitions;\r\n* artifact position;\r\n* page curl;\r\n* map territory truth;\r\n* server progress;\r\n\n===== 1645 =====\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 22. StPageFlip Ownership\r\n\r\nStPageFlip is the only owner of physical journal page curl.\r\n\r\nIt owns:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nmanual drag turn\r\npointer turn\r\ntouch turn\r\nkeyboard turn\r\nprogrammatic turn\r\npage index\r\nturn direction\r\nphysical curl\r\norientation\r\nsingle/double-page behavior\r\n```\r\n\r\nGSAP may own:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nbefore-turn narrative preparation\r\nafter-commit ink or stamp\r\nafter-settle objective reveal\r\n```\r\n\r\nMotion may own:\r\n\r\n```text\r\ntabs\r\ncontrols\r\nnote presence\r\nsurrounding layout\r\n```\r\n\r\nCSS may own:\r\n\r\n```text\r\npage material\r\nedge shadow\r\npaper texture\r\nhover affordance\r\n```\r\n\r\nDo not recreate page curl with GSAP.\r\n\r\nDo not let Motion animate the StPageFlip transform node.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 23. PageFlip Lifecycle Contract\r\n\r\nCreate and freeze lifecycle events:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nturn-request\r\nturn-start\r\nturn-progress\r\nturn-commit\r\nturn-settle\r\nturn-cancel\r\nturn-failed\r\norientation-start\r\norientation-commit\r\ncontent-update-start\r\ncontent-update-commit\r\nfallback-entered\r\n```\r\n\r\nNot every event must be public if it creates unnecessary coupling, but consumers need stable semantic boundaries.\r\n\r\n## Required payload\r\n\r\nAt minimum:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nbook instance ID\r\nturn ID\r\nsource page\r\ndestination page\r\ndirection\r\ninput type\r\nrequested mode\r\nactual mode\r\norientation\r\ntimestamp\r\nresult\r\n```\r\n\r\nInput type must distinguish:\r\n\r\n```text\r\npointer\r\ntouch\r\nkeyboard\r\nprogrammatic\r\nchapter-tab\r\nreplay-restore\r\norientation-recovery\r\n```\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 24. Full and Gentle Timing\r\n\r\nThe existing PageFlip instance must correctly respond to mode changes.\r\n\r\nExpected defaults from the audit:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nfull: approximately 1100 ms\r\ngentle: approximately 620 ms\r\n```\r\n\r\nUse current approved values if later phases changed them.\r\n\r\n## Required strategy\r\n\r\nPrefer updating PageFlip settings in place if supported.\r\n\r\nIf recreation is necessary:\r\n\r\n1. capture current page;\r\n2. capture orientation;\r\n3. capture pending turn;\r\n4. capture focus target;\r\n5. safely destroy old instance;\r\n6. create one new instance;\r\n7. restore current page;\r\n8. restore orientation;\r\n9. reconcile pending turn;\r\n10. restore focus;\r\n11. verify one visible book;\r\n12. verify no stale clone.\r\n\r\nNo visible jump or reset to page zero is allowed.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 25. Product and Browser Reduced Motion\r\n\r\nWhen resolved policy is reduced:\r\n\r\n* do not use physical curl;\r\n* use an accessible static page swap;\r\n* preserve current page;\r\n* preserve reading order;\r\n* preserve tab state;\r\n* preserve focus;\r\n* preserve buttons;\r\n* preserve page announcement;\r\n* preserve chapter semantics.\r\n\r\nReduced mode must not:\r\n\r\n* remove content;\r\n* hide navigation;\r\n* trap users on one page;\r\n* replay the journal opening;\r\n* mount both static and physical books accessibly.\r\n\r\nOnly one visible and accessible content copy may exist.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 26. Manual, Keyboard, Drag, and Programmatic Turns\r\n\r\nAll input methods must share one authoritative lifecycle.\r\n\r\n## Manual pointer/touch drag\r\n\r\n* edge intent appears;\r\n* drag begins only from valid area;\r\n* physical curl remains StPageFlip-owned;\r\n* cancellation restores stable page;\r\n* no false commit.\r\n\r\n## Keyboard\r\n\r\nSupport approved keys and controls.\r\n\r\n* no focus trap;\r\n* no duplicate turn;\r\n* live page announcement after commit;\r\n* reduced fallback remains keyboard operable.\r\n\r\n## Programmatic turn\r\n\r\n* queue only one valid pending destination or use a documented queue;\r\n* ignore duplicate destination;\r\n* reject invalid page;\r\n* never fight a manual turn;\r\n* emit the same commit and settle events.\r\n\r\n## Chapter-tab turn\r\n\r\n* Motion tab state may indicate pending destination;\r\n* active tab changes authoritatively at commit;\r\n* failure restores the prior active tab.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 27. PageFlip Source and Clone Integrity\r\n\r\nConfirm the Phase 2 source/clone boundary remains intact.\r\n\r\nRequired:\r\n\r\n* hidden React source is not focusable;\r\n* hidden source is absent from the accessibility tree;\r\n* hidden source cannot satisfy cinematic target contracts;\r\n* visible clone has current page identity;\r\n* stale clone cannot qualify;\r\n* duplicate IDs do not exist;\r\n* source and clone attributes are sanitized or namespaced;\r\n* content update does not leak prior clones;\r\n* one PageFlip runtime owns one visible book.\r\n\r\nTest the high-risk fixture containing:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nhidden source\r\nvisible current clone\r\nvisible noncurrent clone\r\nstale clone\r\npermanent section target\r\ntemporary event target\r\n```\r\n\r\nOnly the declared visible target may qualify.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 28. Page Preservation\r\n\r\nPreserve current page through:\r\n\r\n* content update;\r\n* chapter release;\r\n* annotation addition;\r\n* side-quest filtering where PageFlip is used;\r\n* orientation change;\r\n* viewport resize;\r\n* full/gentle switch;\r\n* temporary runtime recreation;\r\n* route resume;\r\n* replay restoration;\r\n* error fallback;\r\n* return from artifact inspection.\r\n\r\nIf the exact page no longer exists, apply a documented clamp policy and announce the result.\r\n\r\nDo not silently jump to the beginning.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 29. Page-Turn Audio\r\n\r\nTrigger audio at lifecycle boundaries.\r\n\r\nRecommended:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nturn-start -> optional paper tension\r\nturn-commit -> page-turn cue\r\nturn-settle -> optional light paper settle\r\n```\r\n\r\nDo not play page-turn audio at request time if the turn later fails.\r\n\r\nSound must:\r\n\r\n* follow user audio preference;\r\n* remain optional;\r\n* not be required to know the page changed;\r\n* not double-fire for one turn;\r\n* not fire in static reduced swap unless approved.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 30. Page Edge and Drag Affordances\r\n\r\nImplement accepted Phase 5 journal-page effects from the OA and Codex ledgers.\r\n\r\nAt minimum examine:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nOA-091 Page-edge hover lift\r\nOA-092 Drag-intent affordance\r\nOA-093 Page-turn midpoint shadow\r\nOA-094 Chapter-tab-to-page travel\r\nOA-095 Queued-turn indicator\r\n```\r\n\r\nCorrect ownership:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nCSS:\r\n- page material\r\n- edge light\r\n- subtle hover\r\n\r\nStPageFlip:\r\n- curl and midpoint geometry\r\n\r\nMotion:\r\n- tab and queued-state interface\r\n\r\nWeb Audio:\r\n- commit cue\r\n```\r\n\r\nDo not add hover-only information.\r\n\r\nTouch users need an equivalent affordance through visible controls or edge hinting.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 31. Journal Page Enhancement Sweep\r\n\r\nImplement every accepted Phase 5 requirement from:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nOA-096 through OA-105\r\n```\r\n\r\naccording to the reconciliation ledger.\r\n\r\nPotential ownership:\r\n\r\n## New chapter divider ribbon\r\n\r\n* Motion or CSS for presence;\r\n* PageFlip page identity;\r\n* no global GSAP query.\r\n\r\n## Handwritten annotation\r\n\r\n* GSAP semantic ink or controlled Lottie segment;\r\n* visible page host only.\r\n\r\n## Illustration reveal\r\n\r\n* Motion or GSAP child reveal after page settle;\r\n* no mount autoplay before page is visible.\r\n\r\n## Bookmark movement\r\n\r\n* Motion layout or CSS material state;\r\n* current page remains authoritative.\r\n\r\n## Objective highlight\r\n\r\n* Motion/CSS;\r\n* no page curl ownership.\r\n\r\n## Hint foldout\r\n\r\n* Motion;\r\n* focus and disclosure semantics preserved.\r\n\r\n## Read/unread ink\r\n\r\n* CSS/Motion state;\r\n* not communicated by color alone.\r\n\r\n## Completed chapter stamp\r\n\r\n* GSAP bounded stamp child;\r\n* StPageFlip page remains stable.\r\n\r\n## Historical watermark\r\n\r\n* CSS/opacity;\r\n* archive state remains readable.\r\n\r\n## Locked-page shimmer\r\n\r\n* restrained CSS/Rive decorative child;\r\n* never implies inaccessible hidden story content exists when it does not.\r\n\r\nEvery accepted item must receive:\r\n\r\n* implementation;\r\n* reduced alternative;\r\n* test;\r\n* matrix status;\r\n* production screenshot checkpoint where appropriate.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 32. Journal Opening and PageFlip Handoff\r\n\r\nThe physical opening sequence must hand off seamlessly into StPageFlip.\r\n\r\nRequired sequence:\r\n\r\n1. atmospheric introduction reaches closed-book state;\r\n2. clasp wakes;\r\n3. latch releases;\r\n4. cover opens;\r\n5. sealed page appears;\r\n6. seal breaks;\r\n7. page stack settles;\r\n8. PageFlip visible host is ready;\r\n9. visual geometry aligns;\r\n10. focus moves to the correct interactive destination;\r\n11. objective note and controls enter;\r\n12. old opening actors clean up without snapback.\r\n\r\n## Geometry continuity\r\n\r\nThe final CSS/GSAP opening book pose must visually match:\r\n\r\n* PageFlip width;\r\n* PageFlip height;\r\n* page angle;\r\n* spine position;\r\n* shadow;\r\n* page-stack offset;\r\n* responsive orientation.\r\n\r\nNo visible book teleport is acceptable.\r\n\r\n## Fallback\r\n\r\nIf PageFlip fails:\r\n\r\n* remain on the readable static book;\r\n* preserve current chapter and page;\r\n* expose navigation controls;\r\n* announce fallback only when useful;\r\n* do not replay the opening.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 33. Invitation Ceremony Integration Gate\r\n\r\nPhase 4 created or upgraded the modern Invitation Ceremony.\r\n\r\nPhase 5 must replace fallback-only object behavior with the production seal.\r\n\r\nVerify the complete sequence:\r\n\r\n1. invitation loading;\r\n2. seal idle;\r\n3. invitation resolved;\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

  data-rive-runtime-status={reportedStatus ?? \"pending\"}\r\n      data-rive-state={semantics.pose}\r\n      data-rive-state-value={semantics.stateValue}\r\n \"3.5%\",\r\n        width: \"clamp(76px, 14%, 150px)\",\r\n        aspectRatio: \"1\",\r\n        pointerEvents: \"none\",\r\n        opacity: mode === \"reduced\" ? 0.72 : 0.88,\r\n      }}\r\n    >\r\n      <RiveStatefulObject\r\n        asset={riveAssets.voyageCompass}\r\n        mode={mode}\r\n        label={`Voyage compass, ${semantics.pose}`}\r\n        signal={activeSignal}\r\n        reducedMotion={{\r\n          stablePose: riveAssets.voyageCompass.reducedPose,\r\n          allowedSemanticSignals: riveAssets.voyageCompass.reducedSemanticSignals,\r\n        }}\r\n        onStatus={handleRuntimeStatus}\r\n      />\r\n    </div>\r\n  );\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function VoyageChart(props: VoyageChartProps) {\r\n  const headingId = useId();\r\n\r\n  return (\r\n publishStatus(status);\r\n    },\r\n    [publishStatus, signalIndex],\r\n  );\r\n\r\n  useEffect(() => {\r\n    if (signalIndex !== 1 || !pendingTerminalStatus.current) return;\r\n    const === 0 ? \"state\" : \"state,progress\"}\r\n      data-rive-reduced-pose={JSON.stringify(riveAssets.finaleMechanism.reducedPose)}\r\n    >\r\n      <RiveStatefulObject\r\n        asset={riveAssets.finaleMechanism}\r\n        mode={mode}\r\n        label={`Finale mechanism, ${pose}; ${completed} of ${total} progress`}\r\n        signal={activeSignal}\r\n        reducedMotion={{\r\n          stablePose: riveAssets.finaleMechanism.reducedPose,\r\n          allowedSemanticSignals: riveAssets.finaleMechanism.reducedSemanticSignals,\r\n        }}\r\n        onStatus={handleRuntimeStatus}\r\n      />\r\n    </div>\r\n  );\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function FinaleChamber({\r\n  snapshot,\r\n  mode,\r\n  progressEventType,\r\n  progressEventId,\r\n authoritativeFinalePose(snapshot.finale.state);\r\n  const completed = snapshot.finale.requirements.reduce((sum, item) => sum + Math.min(item.current, item.target), 0);\r\n  const total = snapshot.finale.requirements.reduce((sum, item) => sum + item.target, 0);\r\n  const progress = total > 0 ? completed / total : 0;\r\n  const stateValue = riveAssets.finaleMechanism.states.indexOf(pose);\r\n  const fallbackSemantics = finaleFallbackSemantics[pose];\r\n  const mechanismLifecycleIdentity = [\r\n    snapshot.campaign.slug,\r\n    progressEventId ?? `snapshot-${snapshot.sequence}`,\r\n    progressEventType ?? \"authoritative\",\r\n    progressRequirementKey ?? \"mechanism\",\r\n    `pose-${pose}`,\r\n    `progress-${completed}-${total}`,\r\n  ].join(\":\");\r\n  const changedRequirement = progressRequirementKey\r\n    ? snapshot.finale.requirements.find((item) => item.key === progressRequirementKey)\r\n    : undefined;\r\n  const presentationState =\r\n    progressEventType === \"FINALE_TEASED\"\r\n      ? \"tease-settled\"\r\n      : progressEventType === \"FINALE_REQUIREMENT_UPDATED\"\r\n        ? \"requirement-update-settled\"\r\n        : \"authoritative-settled\";\r\n  const readableStatus =\r\n    progressEventType === \"FINALE_REQUIREMENT_UPDATED\" && changedRequirement\r\n      ? `${changedRequirement.label}: ${changedRequirement.current} of ${changedRequirement.target}. Mechanism state ${pose}.`\r\n      : progressEventType === \"FINALE_TEASED\"\r\n        ? `The final seal is awake and remains ${pose}.`\r\n        : `Mechanism state ${pose}. ${completed} of ${total} progress steps are visible.`;\r\n  return (\r\n    <SceneHost\r\n      as=\"section\"\r\n      kind=\"player-section-enhancement\"\r\n      className=\"physical-section finale-chamber\"\r\n      aria-labelledby={headingId}\r\n      data-section-heading\r\n      data-finale-authoritative-state={snapshot.finale.state.trim().toUpperCase() || \"DORMANT\"}\r\n      data-finale-pose={pose}\r\n      data-finale-semantic-label={fallbackSemantics.label}\r\n      data-finale-progress={progress.toFixed(3)}\r\n      data-finale-reduced-equivalent=\"semantic-final-state\"\r\n      data-finale-presentation-state={presentationState}\r\n      tabIndex={-1}\r\n    >\r\n      <header className=\"section-masthead\">\r\n        <div>\r\n          <p className=\"eyebrow\">{snapshot.finale.state.replaceAll(\"_\", \" \")}</p>\r\n          <h2 id={headingId}>The Final Seal</h2>\r\n        </div>\r\n        <p>{snapshot.finale.teaser ?? \"The inner chamber remains structurally present and safely unreadable.\"}</p>\r\n      </header>\r\n      <p className=\"finale-readable-status\" data-finale-readable-status>\r\n        {readableStatus}\r\n {fallbackSemantics.description} {completed} of {total} progress steps are visible.\r\n        </span>\r\n      </p>\r\n      <div className=\"celestial-chamber\" data-finale-progress={progress.toFixed(3)}>\r\n        <div className=\"constellation-field\" aria-hidden=\"true\">\r\n          {Array.from({ length: 32 }, (_, index) => (\r\n            <i key={index} />\r\n          ))}\r\n        </div>\r\n        <LottieEffect\r\n          asset={lottieAssets.rollingFog}\r\n          mode={mode}\r\n          label=\"Celestial fog around the dormant finale mechanism\"\r\n          className=\"finale-fog\"\r\n        />\r\n        <div className=\"finale-machine\">\r\n          <StaticFinaleMechanism\r\n            key={`static-${mechanismLifecycleIdentity}`}\r\n            pose={pose}\r\n            progress={progress}\r\n            mode={mode}\r\n            report={onTargetRegistrationChange}\r\n pose={pose}\r\n            stateValue={stateValue}\r\n            progress={progress}\r\n            completed={completed}\r\n            total={total}\r\n            nonce={snapshot.sequence}\r\n            onStatusChange={onMechanismStatusChange}\r\n          />\r\n        </div>\r\n        <ul className=\"finale-requirements\">\r\n          {snapshot.finale.requirements.map((item) => (\r\n            <FinaleRequirementRow\r\n              key={item.key}\r\n              item={item}\r\n              isProgressRequirement={\r\n                progressEventType === \"FINALE_REQUIREMENT_UPDATED\" && item.key === progressRequirementKey\r\n              }\r\n              report={onTargetRegistrationChange}\r\n            />\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

\"number\" && Number.isFinite(value)) {\r\n    input.value = value;\r\n    return true;\r\n  }\r\n  return false;\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function RiveRuntime(props: RiveRuntimeProps) {\r\n  const { asset, label, className } = props;\r\n  const [failedAssetKey, setFailedAssetKey] = useState<string | null>(null);\r\n  if (failedAssetKey === asset.key)\r\n    return <FailedRiveFallback asset={asset} label={label} className={className} onStatus={props.onStatus} />;\r\n  return <LiveRiveRuntime key={asset.key} {...props} onFailure={() => setFailedAssetKey(asset.key)} />;\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction FailedRiveFallback({\r\n  asset,\r\n  label,\r\n  className,\r\n  onStatus,\r\n}: Pick<RiveRuntimeProps, \"asset\" | \"label\" | \"className\" | \"onStatus\">) {\r\n  useEffect(() => {\r\n   className={className}\r\n    />\r\n  );\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction LiveRiveRuntime({\r\n  asset,\r\n  mode,\r\n  label,\r\n  className,\r\n  signal,\r\n  signals,\r\n  motionPolicy,\r\n useRef(onInputs);\r\n  const onStatusRef = useRef(onStatus);\r\n  const onFailureRef = useRef(onFailure);\r\n  const failureReported = useRef(false);\r\n  const loaded = useRef(false);\r\n  const return () => changeMountedMetric(\"rive\", -1);\r\n  }, [rive]);\r\n\r\n  useEffect(() => {\r\n    if (!rive) return;\r\n    const inputs = rive.stateMachineInputs(asset.stateMachine) as page.eyebrow ?? page.kind,\r\n        content: <JournalPageContent page={page} />,\r\n      })),\r\n    [pages],\r\n  );\r\n  const payload = activeEvent?.type === \"CHAPTER_RELEASED\" ? activeEvent.payload : null;\r\n  const annotationTarget = useMemo(\r\n    () => resolveJournalAnnotationTarget(activeEvent, pages, pageTargets),\r\n    [activeEvent, pageTargets, pages],\r\n  );\r\n  const claspPose = journalClaspPoseByPhase[openingPhase];\r\n  const claspStateValue = riveAssets.journalClasp.states.indexOf(claspPose);\r\n  const claspLifecycleIdentity = [\r\n    snapshot.campaign.slug,\r\n    activeEvent?.id ?? `snapshot-${snapshot.sequence}`,\r\n    openingPhase,\r\n  ].join(\":\");\r\n  const handleJournalStageTarget = useCallback((handle: SceneTargetHandle | null) => {\r\n    setJournalStageTarget(handle);\r\n  }, []);\r\n  const handleCeremonyTargets = useCallback((handles: CeremonyOverlayTargetHandles | null) => {\r\n    setCeremonyTargets(handles);\r\n  }, []);\r\n  const ready = useMemo<JournalCeremonyTargetReady | null>(() => {\r\n    if (!ceremonyHost || !journalStageTarget || !ceremonyTargets) return null;\r\n    return Object.freeze({\r\n      host: ceremonyHost,\r\n      targets: Object.freeze({\r\n        \"journal-stage\": Object.freeze([journalStageTarget]),\r\n        ...ceremonyTargets,\r\n      }),\r\n    });\r\n  }, [ceremonyHost, ceremonyTargets, journalStageTarget]);\r\n\r\n  useEffect(() => {\r\n    onSceneHostChange?.(ready?.host ?? null);\r\n    onSceneTargetsChange?.(ready);\r\n    return () => {\r\n      onSceneHostChange?.(null);\r\n      onSceneTargetsChange?.(null);\r\n    };\r\n  }, [onSceneHostChange, onSceneTargetsChange, ready]);\r\n\r\n  useEffect(() => {\r\n    onAnnotationTargetChange?.(annotationTarget);\r\n    return () => onAnnotationTargetChange?.(null);\r\n  }, [annotationTarget, onAnnotationTargetChange]);\r\n\r\n  return (\r\n    <SceneHost\r\n      as=\"section\"\r\n      kind=\"player-progression\"\r\n      hostKey={`journal-chapter-ceremony-${snapshot.campaign.slug}`}\r\n      className=\"physical-section journal-workspace\"\r\n      aria-labelledby=\"journal-heading\"\r\n      data-section-heading\r\n      data-journal-phase={openingPhase}\r\n      tabIndex={-1}\r\n    >\r\n      <SceneHostHandleBridge onChange={setCeremonyHost} />\r\n      <header className=\"section-masthead\">\r\n        <div>\r\n          <p className=\"eyebrow\">Primary story artifact</p>\r\n          <h2 id=\"journal-heading\">The Voyage Journal</h2>\r\n        </div>\r\n        <p>Selectable parchment, physical leaves, and only the words the tide has released.</p>\r\n      </header>\r\n      <PhysicalJournalBook\r\n        ref={book}\r\n        pages={flipPages}\r\n        mode={mode}\r\n        openingPhase={openingPhase}\r\n        interactive={interactive}\r\n        playbackRate={playbackRate}\r\n        revision={snapshot.sequence}\r\n        initialPage={pageIndexForChapter(pages, snapshot.chapter.ordinal)}\r\n        coverTitle=\"The Forever Treasure\"\r\n        coverSubtitle=\"Captain's Voyage Journal\"\r\n        tabs={snapshot.chapters.map((chapter) => ({\r\n          id: String(chapter.ordinal),\r\n          ordinal: chapter.ordinal,\r\n          label: chapter.title ?? chapter.teaser ?? \"Sealed\",\r\n          state: chapter.state.toLowerCase(),\r\n          pageIndex: pageIndexForChapter(pages, chapter.ordinal),\r\n        }))}\r\n        onSelectTab={(page) => book.current?.flipTo(page)}\r\n        onPageTurn={onPageTurn}\r\n        onPageTargetsChange={setPageTargets}\r\n        onJournalStageTargetChange={handleJournalStageTarget}\r\n        overlay={\r\n          <>\r\n            <JournalClaspAdapter\r\n              key={claspLifecycleIdentity}\r\n              mode={mode}\r\n              pose={claspPose}\r\n              stateValue={claspStateValue}\r\n              nonce={snapshot.sequence}\r\n              onStatusChange={onClaspStatusChange}\r\n            />\r\n            {payload && interactive ? (\r\n              <ChapterCeremonyPage\r\n                eventId={activeEvent!.id}\r\n                payload={payload}\r\n                mode={mode}\r\n                onTargetsChange={handleCeremonyTargets}\r\n                onInkCommandChange={onChapterInkCommandChange}\r\n              />\r\n            ) : null}\r\n          </>\r\n        }\r\n      />\r\n    </SceneHost>\r\n  );\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction JournalClaspAdapter({\r\n  mode,\r\n  pose,\r\n  stateValue,\r\n  nonce,\r\n  onStatusChange,\r\n}: Readonly<{\r\n  mode: MotionMode;\r\n  pose: JournalClaspPose;\r\n  stateValue: number;\r\n  nonce: number;\r\n  onStatusChange?: (status: RiveRuntimeStatus | null) => void;\r\n}>) {\r\n  const [reportedStatus, setReportedStatus] = useState<RiveRuntimeStatus | null>(null);\r\n  const statusRef = useRef<RiveRuntimeStatus | null>(null);\r\n  const callbackRef = useRef(onStatusChange);\r\n\r\n  useEffect(() => {\r\n    const previous = callbackRef.current;\r\n    if (previous === onStatusChange) return;\r\n    previous?.(null);\r\n    callbackRef.current = onStatusChange;\r\n    if (statusRef.current) onStatusChange?.(statusRef.current);\r\n  }, [onStatusChange]);\r\n\r\n  useEffect(\r\n    () => () => {\r\n      callbackRef.current?.(null);\r\n      callbackRef.current = undefined;\r\n      statusRef.current = null;\r\n    },\r\n    [],\r\n  );\r\n\r\n  const handleStatus = useCallback((status: RiveRuntimeStatus) => {\r\n    statusRef.current = status;\r\n    setReportedStatus(status);\r\n    callbackRef.current?.(status);\r\n  }, []);\r\n\r\n  return (\r\n    <>\r\n      <p className=\"sr-only\" data-journal-clasp-readable-status data-journal-clasp-state={pose}>\r\n        {journalClaspReadableState[pose]}\r\n      </p>\r\n      <div\r\n        aria-hidden=\"true\"\r\n        data-journal-clasp-contract\r\n        data-rive-contract-availability={riveAssets.journalClasp.availability}\r\n        data-rive-runtime-status={reportedStatus ?? \"pending\"}\r\n        data-rive-state={pose}\r\n        data-rive-state-value={stateValue}\r\n        data-rive-inputs={riveAssets.journalClasp.inputs.map((input) => input.name).join(\",\")}\r\n        data-rive-reduced-pose={JSON.stringify(riveAssets.journalClasp.reducedPose)}\r\n        data-rive-reduced-equivalent=\"semantic-final-state\"\r\n        data-rive-production-art-status={riveAssets.journalClasp.availability}\r\n        style={{\r\n          position: \"absolute\",\r\n          zIndex: 31,\r\n          top: \"245px\",\r\n          left: \"calc(50% + 130px)\",\r\n          width: \"96px\",\r\n          height: \"96px\",\r\n          pointerEvents: \"none\",\r\n          opacity: pose === \"open\" ? 0.4 : 0.82,\r\n        }}\r\n      >\r\n        <RiveStatefulObject\r\n          asset={riveAssets.journalClasp}\r\n          mode={mode}\r\n          label={`Journal clasp, ${pose}`}\r\n          signal={{ name: \"state\", value: stateValue, nonce }}\r\n          reducedMotion={{\r\n            stablePose: riveAssets.journalClasp.reducedPose,\r\n            allowedSemanticSignals: riveAssets.journalClasp.reducedSemanticSignals,\r\n          }}\r\n          onStatus={handleStatus}\r\n        />\r\n      </div>\r\n    </>\r\n  );\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction SceneHostHandleBridge({ onChange }: { onChange: (host: SceneHostHandle | null) => void }) {\r\n  const host = useOptionalSceneHost();\r\n  useEffect(() => {\r\n    onChange(host);\r\n    return () => onChange(null);\r\n  }, [host, onChange]);\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

}[stage];\n  return (\n    <motion.section\n      key={stage}\n      className={`platform-state-card invitation-state invitation-state-${stage}`}\n      initial={{ opacity: 0, y: stage === === \"replaced\" ? \"Check for replacement\" : \"Try again\"}\n          </button>\n        )}\n        {stage === \"account-required\" && (\n          <Link className=\"brass-button\" href=\"/player/sign-in\">\n            Sign in, then return here\n          </Link>\n        )}\n        <Link className=\"button-subtle\" href=\"/player/sign-in\">\n          Return to Player Entry\n        </Link>\n      </div>\n    </motion.section>\n  );\n}\n\nexport function InvitationCeremony({ onRouteHandoff }: { onRouteHandoff?: InvitationRouteHandoff } = {}) {\n  const router = useRouter();\n  const search = useSearchParams();\n  const root = useRef<HTMLElement>(null);\n  const ceremonyHost = useRef<SceneHostHandle | null>(null);\n  const resolveRun = useRef<AbortController | null>(null);\n  const { director } = useAnimationDirector();\n  const { mode } = useMotionMode();\n  const asyncState = useAuthoritativeAsyncState(900);\n  const invalidState = search.get(\"state\") === \"invalid\";\n  const [resolvedStage, setStage] = useState<InvitationStage>(\"resolving\");\n  const [invitation, setInvitation] = useState<Invitation | null>(null);\n  const [csrf, setCsrf] = useState(\"\");\n  const [pin, setPin] = useState(\"\");\n  const [displayName, setDisplayName] = useState(\"\");\n  const [error, setError] = useState(\"\");\n  const [announcement, setAnnouncement] = useState(\"Checking invitation status.\");\n  const [coverVisible, setCoverVisible] = useState(true);\n  const stateToken = resolvePlatformMotionToken(\"state\", mode);\n  const ceremonyToken = resolvePlatformMotionToken(\"ceremony\", mode);\n  const stage: InvitationStage = invalidState ? \"invalid\" : resolvedStage;\n  const visibleError = invalidState ? \"This invitation is invalid or no longer available.\" : error;\n\n  const resolveInvitation = useCallback(async (replacement = false) => {\n    resolveRun.current?.abort(\"superseded\");\n    const controller = new AbortController();\n    resolveRun.current = controller;\n    setStage(replacement ? \"replacing\" : \"resolving\");\n    setError(\"\");\n    try {\n      const response = await fetch(\"/api/invitations/resolve\", { cache: \"no-store\", signal: controller.signal });\n      const body = (await response.json().catch(() => ({}))) as {\n        invitation?: Invitation;\n        csrfToken?: string;\n        error?: string;\n        code?: string;\n      };\n      if (!response.ok || !body.invitation) {\n        const next = stageFromCode(body.code, false);\n        setStage(next);\n        setError(body.error ?? \"This invitation is not available.\");\n        setAnnouncement(body.error ?? \"This invitation is not available.\");\n        return;\n      }\n      setInvitation(body.invitation);\n      setCsrf(body.csrfToken ?? \"\");\n      setDisplayName(body.invitation.recipientName);\n      setCoverVisible(true);\n      const next = body.invitation.requiresPin ? \"pin-required\" : \"valid\";\n      setStage(next);\n      setAnnouncement(\n        body.invitation.requiresPin\n          ? \"Invitation found. Enter its PIN to continue.\"\n          : \"Invitation found and ready to accept.\",\n      );\n    } catch (cause) {\n      if (cause instanceof DOMException && cause.name === \"AbortError\") return;\n      setStage(\"failed\");\n      setError(\"This invitation could not be reached. Check your connection and try again.\");\n      setAnnouncement(\"Invitation lookup failed.\");\n    } finally {\n      if (resolveRun.current === controller) resolveRun.current = null;\n    }\n  }, []);\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (invalidState) return;\n    const timer = window.setTimeout(() => void resolveInvitation(), 0);\n    return () => window.clearTimeout(timer);\n  }, [invalidState, resolveInvitation]);\n\n  useEffect(() => () => resolveRun.current?.abort(\"unmounted\"), []);\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    const timer = window.setTimeout(\n      () => document.getElementById(\"invitation-state-title\")?.focus(),\n      stateToken.durationMs + 20,\n    );\n    return () => window.clearTimeout(timer);\n  }, [stage, stateToken.durationMs]);\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (!invitation) return;\n    const remaining = new Date(invitation.expiresAt).getTime() - Date.now();\n    const timer = window.setTimeout(\n      () => {\n        setStage(\"expired\");\n        setAnnouncement(\"This invitation has expired.\");\n      },\n      Math.min(Math.max(remaining, 0), 2_147_483_647),\n    );\n    return () => window.clearTimeout(timer);\n  }, [invitation]);\n\n  async function handOffRoute(destination: string, signal: AbortSignal) {\n    if (onRouteHandoff) return onRouteHandoff(destination, signal);\n    router.push(destination);\n    router.refresh();\n  }\n\n  function restoreFailure(run: AuthoritativeAsyncRun, message: string, code?: string) {\n    if (!asyncState.fail(run)) return;\n    const next = stageFromCode(code, Boolean(invitation));\n    if (next === \"pin-required\") setPin(\"\");\n    setStage(next);\n    setError(message);\n    setAnnouncement(message);\n  }\n\n  async function act(action: \"accept\" | \"decline\") {\n    if (!invitation || !root.current) return;\n    if (action === \"decline\" && !window.confirm(\"Decline this invitation? The Captain will see your response.\")) return;\n    const run = asyncState.begin();\n    if (!run) return;\n    const pendingStage = action === \"decline\" ? \"declining\" : invitation.requiresPin ? \"pin-validating\" : \"accepting\";\n    setStage(pendingStage);\n    setError(\"\");\n    setAnnouncement(\n      action === \"decline\" ? \"Declining invitation.\" : \"Validating invitation with the Captain's ledger.\",\n    );\n    let operationError = `Unable to ${action} this invitation.`;\n    let operationCode: string | undefined;\n    let actionPromise: Promise<InvitationActionResult> | null = null;\n    const submitAction = () => {\n      actionPromise ??= (async () => {\n        const response = await fetch(`/api/invitations/${action}`, {\n          method: \"POST\",\n          headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\", \"x-csrf-token\": csrf },\n          body: JSON.stringify(action === \"accept\" ? { pin, displayName } : {}),\n          signal: run.controller.signal,\n        });\n        const body = (await response.json().catch(() => ({}))) as {\n          error?: string;\n          code?: string;\n          playthroughId?: string;\n        };\n        if (!response.ok || (action === \"accept\" && !body.playthroughId)) {\n          operationError = body.error ?? operationError;\n          operationCode = body.code;\n          throw new Error(\"invitation-operation-rejected\");\n        }\n        return { ok: true, playthroughId: body.playthroughId };\n      })();\n      return actionPromise;\n    };\n\n    try {\n      if (action === \"decline\") {\n        const result = await submitAction();\n        if (!asyncState.succeed(run) || !result.ok) return;\n        setStage(\"declined\");\n        setAnnouncement(\"Invitation declined. Returning to Player Entry.\");\n        await new Promise((resolve) => window.setTimeout(resolve, stateToken.durationMs));\n        await handOffRoute(\"/player/sign-in\", run.controller.signal);\n        return;\n      }\n      if (!ceremonyHost.current) {\n        restoreFailure(run, \"The invitation ceremony is still preparing. Try again.\");\n        return;\n      }\n      let fallbackResult: InvitationActionResult | undefined;\n      let fallbackReadable = false;\n      const receipt = await director.play<InvitationActionResult>(\"player-access\", {\n        root: root.current,\n        hostId: ceremonyHost.current.hostId,\n        hostKind: ceremonyHost.current.kind,\n        sceneHost: ceremonyHost.current,\n        requestSource: \"operation\",\n        eventOrActionId: invitation.id,\n        queue: false,\n        signal: run.controller.signal,\n        operation: submitAction,\n        finalStateRuntime: {\n          holdSafePose: (semanticState) => {\n            if (semanticState !== accessFinalState || run.controller.signal.aborted) return;\n            setStage(\"accepted\");\n            setAnnouncement(\"Invitation accepted. Opening the waiting room.\");\n          },\n          verifyReadableState: (semanticState) => semanticState === accessFinalState,\n        },\n        presentationFallback: async (context) => {\n          if (context.fallback !== accessFallback || context.signal?.aborted)\n            return { completed: false, readable: false };\n          try {\n            fallbackResult = await submitAction();\n            setStage(\"accepted\");\n            setAnnouncement(\"Invitation accepted. Opening the waiting room.\");\n            fallbackReadable = true;\n            return { completed: true, readable: true, semanticState: accessFinalState };\n          } catch {\n            return { completed: false, readable: false };\n          }\n        },\n      });\n      const result = receipt.operationResult ?? fallbackResult;\n      const presented =\n        result?.ok === true &&\n        (receipt.outcome === \"presented\" ||\n          receipt.outcome === \"skipped-by-user\" ||\n          (receipt.outcome === \"presented-fallback\" && fallbackReadable));\n      if (!presented || !result.playthroughId) {\n        restoreFailure(run, operationError, operationCode);\n        return;\n      }\n      if (!asyncState.succeed(run)) return;\n      setStage(\"accepted\");\n      setAnnouncement(\"Invitation accepted. Opening the waiting room.\");\n      await handOffRoute(`/player/playthroughs/${result.playthroughId}`, run.controller.signal);\n    } catch {\n      restoreFailure(run, operationError, operationCode);\n    }\n  }\n\n  const terminal =\n    !invitation ||\n    [\n      \"account-required\",\n      \"invalid\",\n      \"expired\",\n      \"revoked\",\n      \"declined\",\n      \"replacing\",\n      \"replaced\",\n      \"failed\",\n      \"resolving\",\n    ].includes(stage);\n  if (terminal) {\n    return (\n      <main className=\"invitation-page\" data-invitation-state={stage} data-motion-mode={mode}>\n        <AnimatePresence mode=\"wait\">\n          <TerminalInvitationState\n            stage={stage as Parameters<typeof TerminalInvitationState>[0][\"stage\"]}\n            detail={visibleError || announcement}\n            retry={() => void resolveInvitation(stage === \"replaced\")}\n          />\n        </AnimatePresence>\n      </main>\n    );\n  }\n\n  const tale = invitation.playthrough.tale;\n  const relicState =\n    stage === \"accepted\" ? \"open\" : stage === \"pin-validating\" || stage === \"accepting\" ? \"accepting\" : \"valid\";\n  return (\n    <main ref={root} className=\"invitation-page\" data-invitation-state={stage} data-motion-mode={mode}>\n      <SceneHost\n        kind=\"access\"\n        hostKey={`invitation-ceremony:${invitation.id}`}\n        className=\"invitation-cinematic-boundary\"\n        aria-hidden=\"true\"\n        style={{ pointerEvents: \"none\" }}\n      >\n        <CeremonyHostBridge mode={mode} onReady={(host) => (ceremonyHost.current = host)} />\n      </SceneHost>\n      <motion.section\n        className=\"invitation-sheet\"\n        aria-labelledby=\"invitation-state-title\"\n        aria-busy={asyncState.busy}\n        initial={{\n          opacity: 0,\n          rotateX: mode === \"reduced\" ? 0 : -8,\n          clipPath: mode === \"reduced\" ? \"inset(0)\" : \"inset(0 0 42% 0 round 18px)\",\n        }}\n        animate={{ opacity: 1, rotateX: 0, clipPath: \"inset(0 0 0% 0 round 18px)\" }}\n        transition={{ duration: stateToken.durationSeconds, ease: platformMotionEasing(\"state\") }}\n      >\n        <div className=\"invitation-seal\" aria-hidden=\"true\">\n          <PlatformRelic kind=\"invitation-seal\" state={relicState} mode={mode} />\n        </div>\n        <div\n          className=\"invitation-cover-frame\"\n          data-cover-state={coverVisible && tale?.coverUrl ? \"image\" : \"fallback\"}\n        >\n          {coverVisible && tale?.coverUrl ? (\n            <motion.img\n              className=\"invitation-cover\"\n              src={tale.coverUrl}\n              alt=\"\"\n              onError={() => setCoverVisible(false)}\n              initial={{ clipPath: mode === \"reduced\" ? \"inset(0)\" : \"inset(0 100% 0 0)\" }}\n              animate={{ clipPath: \"inset(0 0% 0 0)\" }}\n              transition={{ duration: stateToken.durationSeconds, ease: platformMotionEasing(\"state\") }}\n            />\n          ) : (\n            <span aria-hidden=\"true\">Charted voyage</span>\n          )}\n        </div>\n        <p className=\"eyebrow\">\n          A Captain&apos;s invitation for{\" \"}\n          <span className=\"invitation-handwritten-name\">{invitation.recipientName}</span>\n        </p>\n        <h1 id=\"invitation-state-title\" tabIndex={-1}>\n          {tale?.title ?? \"A Tall Tale awaits\"}\n        </h1>\n        <h2>{invitation.playthrough.voyageName}</h2>\n        <p>{tale?.shortDescription ?? \"Your voyage is ready to join.\"}</p>\n        <dl>\n          <div>\n            <dt>Edition</dt>\n            <dd>{invitation.playthrough.versionLabel ?? \"Published edition\"}</dd>\n          </div>\n          <div>\n            <dt>Invitation expires</dt>\n            <dd>{new Date(invitation.expiresAt).toLocaleString()}</dd>\n          </div>\n          {invitation.playthrough.plannedStartAt && (\n            <div>\n              <dt>Planned start</dt>\n              <dd>\n                {new Date(invitation.playthrough.plannedStartAt).toLocaleString()}{\" \"}\n                {invitation.playthrough.scheduleTimezone}\n              </dd>\n            </div>\n          )}\n        </dl>\n        <label>\n          <span>Your display name</span>\n          <input\n            value={displayName}\n            onChange={(event) => setDisplayName(event.target.value)}\n            required\n            disabled={asyncState.busy}\n          />\n        </label>\n        {invitation.requiresPin && (\n          <label>\n            <span>Invitation PIN</span>\n            <input\n              aria-label=\"Invitation PIN\"\n              type=\"password\"\n              inputMode=\"numeric\"\n              autoComplete=\"one-time-code\"\n              value={pin}\n              onChange={(event) => setPin(event.target.value.replace(/\\D/g, \"\"))}\n              required\n              disabled={asyncState.busy}\n              aria-describedby=\"invitation-pin-progress\"\n            />\n            <span id=\"invitation-pin-progress\" className=\"pin-progress\" aria-live=\"polite\">\n              {Array.from({ length: 4 }, (_, index) => (\n                <i key={index} data-filled={index < pin.length} aria-hidden=\"true\" />\n              ))}\n              <b className=\"sr-only\">{Math.min(pin.length, 4)} PIN digits entered</b>\n            </span>\n          </label>\n        )}\n        {visibleError && (\n          <p className=\"platform-error\" role=\"alert\">\n            {visibleError}\n          </p>\n        )}\n        <AnimatePresence mode=\"wait\">\n          <motion.p\n            key={stage}\n            className=\"platform-status invitation-live-state\"\n            role=\"status\"\n            aria-live=\"polite\"\n            initial={{ opacity: 0 }}\n            animate={{ opacity: 1 }}\n            exit={{ opacity: 0 }}\n          >\n            {stage === \"accepted\" ? \"Invitation accepted. The seal is open.\" : announcement}\n          </motion.p>\n        </AnimatePresence>\n        <div className=\"invitation-actions\">\n          <button\n            className=\"brass-button\"\n            disabled={asyncState.busy || !displayName || (invitation.requiresPin && !pin)}\n            aria-busy={asyncState.busy}\n            onClick={() => void act(\"accept\")}\n          >\n            {stage === \"pin-validating\"\n              ? \"Validating PINâ€¦\"\n              : stage === \"accepting\"\n                ? \"Joining voyageâ€¦\"\n                : stage === \"accepted\"\n                  ? \"Invitation accepted\"\n                  : \"Accept and Join Voyage\"}\n          </button>\n          <button className=\"button-subtle\" disabled={asyncState.busy} onClick={() => void act(\"decline\")}>\n            Decline Invitation\n          </button>\n        </div>\n      </motion.section>\n      {stage === \"accepted\" && (\n        <span\n          className=\"invitation-route-hold\"\n          style={{ \"--hold-ms\": `${ceremonyToken.durationMs}ms` } as CSSProperties}\n          aria-hidden=\"true\"\n        />\n      )}\n    </main>\n  );\n}\n\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

Releasing the journal claspâ€¦\"\r\n            : \"Your place is secured. The journal will open only after the Captain launches the voyage.\"}\r\n        </p>\r\n        <dl>\r\n          <div>\r\n            <dt>Edition</dt>\r\n            <dd>{voyage.versionLabel}</dd>\r\n          </div>\r\n          <div>\r\n            <dt>Readiness</dt>\r\n            <dd>\r\n              {launchReady ? \"Launch confirmed\" : voyage.status === \"SCHEDULED\" ? \"Scheduled\" : \"Awaiting Captain\"}\r\n            </dd>\r\n          </div>\r\n          {voyage.plannedStartAt && (\r\n            <div data-planned-start-due={plannedCountdown === \"0m 0s\"}>\r\n              <dt>Planned start</dt>\r\n              <dd>\r\n                {new Date(voyage.plannedStartAt).toLocaleString()} Â· <span role=\"timer\">{plannedCountdown}</span>\r\n              </dd>\r\n            </div>\r\n          )}\r\n          <div>\r\n            <dt>Connection</dt>\r\n            <dd className={`connection-${connection}`} role=\"status\" aria-live=\"polite\">\r\n              {connectionCopy[connection]}\r\n            </dd>\r\n          </div>\r\n          <div>\r\n            <dt>Last server confirmation</dt>\r\n            <dd>{new Date(voyage.lastSynchronizedAt).toLocaleTimeString()}</dd>\r\n          </div>\r\n        </dl>\r\n        {reconciliation.length > 0 && (\r\n          <section className=\"reconciliation-summary\" aria-labelledby=\"reconciliation-title\">\r\n            <h3 id=\"reconciliation-title\">Changes received while reconnecting</h3>\r\n            <ol>\r\n              {reconciliation.map((change, index) => (\r\n                <li key={`${index}-${change}`}>{change}</li>\r\n              ))}\r\n            </ol>\r\n          </section>\r\n        )}\r\n        <section className=\"crew-readiness\">\r\n          <h3>Crew readiness</h3>\r\n          <motion.ul layout>\r\n            <AnimatePresence initial={false}>\r\n              {voyage.crew.map((member) => {\r\n                const identity = crewIdentity(member);\r\n                return (\r\n                  <motion.li\r\n                    layout\r\n                    key={identity}\r\n                    initial={newCrew.has(identity) ? { opacity: 0, y: layoutToken.distancePx } : false}\r\n                    animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}\r\n                    exit={{ opacity: 0 }}\r\n                    transition={{ duration: layoutToken.durationSeconds, ease: platformMotionEasing(\"layout\") }}\r\n                  >\r\n                    <span>{member.displayName}</span>\r\n                    <small>\r\n                      {member.crewRole ?? \"Player\"} Â· {member.status.replaceAll(\"_\", \" \").toLocaleLowerCase()}\r\n                    </small>\r\n                  </motion.li>\r\n                );\r\n              })}\r\n            </AnimatePresence>\r\n          </motion.ul>\r\n        </section>\r\n        {error && (\r\n          <p className=\"platform-error\" role=\"alert\">\r\n            {error}\r\n          </p>\r\n        )}\r\n        <div className=\"waiting-actions\">\r\n          {launchReady && routeFailed && (\r\n            <button className=\"brass-button\" onClick={() => setRouteFailed(false)}>\r\n              Open launched journal\r\n            </button>\r\n          )}\r\n          {connection !== \"revoked\" && (\r\n            <button\r\n              className=\"button-secondary\"\r\n              disabled={connection === \"reconciling\"}\r\n              onClick={() => {\r\n                setConnection(\"reconnecting\");\r\n                void load(\"live\");\r\n              }}\r\n            >\r\n              Reconnect and Refresh\r\n            </button>\r\n          )}\r\n          <Link className=\"button-subtle\" href=\"/player/library\">\r\n            Leave Waiting Room\r\n          </Link>\r\n        </div>\r\n      </section>\r\n    </main>\r\n  );\r\n}\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

seconds\\nOutput:\\n\\nC:\\\\Users\\\\kgray\\\\AppData\\\\Local\\\\ForeverTreasureCompanion\\\\validation\\\\src\\\\animation\\\\motion\\\\useMotionMode.ts\\n  18:41  error  Error: Calling setState synchronously within an effect can trigger cascading renders\\n\\nEffects are intended to synchronize state between React and external systems such as manually updating the DOM, state management libraries, or other platform APIs. In general, the body of an effect should do one or both of the following:\\n* Update external systems with the latest state from React.\\n* Subscribe for updates from some external system, calling setState in a callback function when external state changes.\\n\\nCalling setState synchronously within an effect body causes cascading renders that can hurt performance, and is not recommended. (https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect).\\n\\nC:\\\\Users\\\\kgray\\\\AppData\\\\Local\\\\ForeverTreasureCompanion\\\\validation\\\\src\\\\animation\\\\motion\\\\useMotionMode.ts:18:41\\n  16 |   useEffect(() => {\\n  17 |     const saved = localStorage.getItem(\\\"forever-motion\\\") as MotionMode | null;\\n> 18 |     if (saved && order.includes(saved)) setProductMode(saved);\\n     |                                         ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ Avoid calling setState() directly within an effect\\n  19 |   }, []);\\n  20 |   useEffect(() => director.setMode(mode), [director, mode]);\\n  21 |   const setMode = useCallback((next: MotionMode) => {  react-hooks/set-state-in-effect\\n\\nC:\\\\Users\\\\kgray\\\\AppData\\\\Local\\\\ForeverTreasureCompanion\\\\validation\\\\src\\\\animation\\\\scenes\\\\access.scene.ts\\n  2:10  warning  'MorphSVGPlugin' is defined but never used  @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars\\n\\nC:\\\\Users\\\\kgray\\\\AppData\\\\Local\\\\ForeverTreasureCompanion\\\\validation\\\\src\\\\animation\\\\scenes\\\\story.scene.ts\\n  2:27  warning  'gsap' is defined but never used  @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars\\n\\nC:\\\\Users\\\\kgray\\\\AppData\\\\Local\\\\ForeverTreasureCompanion\\\\validation\\\\src\\\\components\\\\animation\\\\AssetFallback.tsx\\n  4:14  warning  Using `<img>` could result in slower LCP and higher bandwidth. Consider using `<Image />` from `next/image` or a custom image loader to automatically optimize images. This may incur additional usage or cost from your provider. See: https://nextjs.org/docs/messages/no-img-element  @next/next/no-img-element\\n\\nC:\\\\Users\\\\kgray\\\\AppData\\\\Local\\\\ForeverTreasureCompanion\\\\validation\\\\src\\\\components\\\\animation\\\\PageFlipBook.tsx\\n  106:12  warning  The ref value 'host.current' will likely have changed by the time this effect cleanup function r…24 tokens truncated…eserved output, why replacement is necessary, the replacement lane's complete launch manifest, ownership handoff, affected dependencies, and confirmation that no material result was lost or left unreviewed.\\r\\n 193: \\r\\n 194: When cancelling a redundant or unnecessary lane, report its lane ID, why it became redundant, what useful output was preserved, which lane now owns any remaining responsibility, which locks or slots were released, and confirmation that no unreviewed material result was discarded.\\r\\n 195: \\r\\n 196: ### Grouping and anti-spam safeguard\\r\\n 197: \\r\\n 198: Transparency must remain useful rather than noisy. Group workers launched or completed at approximately the same time into one structured update. Do not require or send updates for every tool call, file opened, search performed, minor internal observation, unchanged status poll, or passage of a few minutes. Do not repeat `still working` messages without a meaningful change, dump full worker prompts unless the user requests them, or expose raw internal reasoning or private chain-of-thought. Announce only meaningful changes in topology, ownership, permissions, status, material findings, dependencies, or integration state.\\r\\n 199: \\r\\n 200: Required disclosure must occur immediately before or after launch through compact grouped manifests and concise meaningful status changes. Communication supports execution and must not become a blocking administrative process: do not delay safe workers merely to produce elaborate chat formatting, duplicate narration, full prompt dumps, or status reports with no new information.\\r\\n 201: \\r\\n 202: ### Final parallel-execution summary\\r\\n 203: \\r\\n 204: For every substantial task that used subagents, the final response must concisely and readably summarize:\\r\\n 205: \\r\\n 206: - total unique subagents used, maximum simultaneous workers, and worker waves;\\r\\n 207: - roles and ownership boundaries, including which lanes were read-only and which modified files;\\r\\n 208: - important results from each lane;\\r\\n 209: - failed, blocked, replaced, or cancelled lanes;\\r\\n 210: - how worker outputs were reviewed, reconciled, integrated, and verified;\\r\\n 211: - whether parallel execution increased capacity, depth, coverage, verification, or reduced elapsed time;\\r\\n 212: - any avoidable duplication or coordination overhead; and\\r\\n 213: - useful improvements for the next task decomposition.\\r\\n 214: \\r\\n 215: Do not reproduce raw worker prompts or private reasoning. Keep the assessment evidence-based, and do not invent numerical percentages when timing or work data was not recorded.\\r\\n 216: \\r\\n 217: ## Mandatory parallelization assessment\\r\\n 218: \\r\\n 219: Every task performs at least a brief parallelization assessment before substantial work begins. A concise internal determination is sufficient only for a genuinely trivial task with no useful independent lane.\\r\\n 220: \\r\\n 221: The assessment identifies:\\r\\n 222: \\r\\n 223: - genuinely independent workstreams and immediately ready read-only discovery;\\r\\n 224: - whether an independent verification lane improves quality;\\r\\n 225: - possible divisions by subsystem, concern, package, route, component family, evidence source, acceptance criterion, test surface, or implementation boundary;\\r\\n 226: - dependency edges, shared files, exclusive resources, and serialized work;\\r\\n 227: - the maximum safe and useful concurrency, worktree needs, integration owner, and final-validation owner.\\r\\n 228: \\r\\n 229: If two or more genuine independent lanes exist, parallel delegation is mandatory unless a concrete technical, safety, dependency, resource, capacity, or ownership reason prevents it. The repository policy itself is standing authorization; the user does not need to request subagents, delegation, workers, parallel execution, or the full available limit.\\r\\n 230: \\r\\n 231: ## Critical-path and fast-start execution\\r\\n 232: \\r\\n 233: Every Tier 1 and Tier 2 plan must identify the task's critical path and use it as an active scheduling input. The coordinator must:\\r\\n 234: \\r\\n 235: - identify which work directly controls final completion time;\\r\\n 236: - launch critical-path work as early as safely possible;\\r\\n 237: - move prerequisite discovery off the critical path through parallel read-only lanes;\\r\\n 238: - prevent noncritical work from blocking critical work;\\r\\n 239: - resolve uncertain or high-risk dependencies early;\\r\\n 240: - begin integration incrementally when safe instead of waiting unnecessarily for every unrelated lane;\\r\\n 241: - start newly unblocked work immediately;\\r\\n 242: - reassign or split a slow lane when doing so can shorten completion time without discarding useful work or reducing quality;\\r\\n 243: - review and integrate completed worker output promptly rather than leaving it waiting; and\\r\\n 244: - prevent the coordinator from becoming the critical-path bottleneck.\\r\\n 245: \\r\\n 246: When multiple valid decompositions exist, prefer the one expected to produce a correct integrated result sooner.\\r\\n 247: \\r\\n 248: ### Fast-start requirement\\r\\n 249: \\r\\n 250: For every nontrivial task:\\r\\n 251: \\r\\n 252: 1. Complete the minimum safety and repository preflight.\\r\\n 253: 2. Identify immediately ready independent lanes.\\r\\n 254: 3. Publish the required subagent manifest.\\r\\n 255: 4. Launch those lanes without unnecessary delay.\\r\\n 256: 5. Continue refining the dependency graph and detailed plan while safe read-only work proceeds.\\r\\n 257: 6. Launch write lanes as soon as ownership, requirements, and prerequisites are sufficiently established.\\r\\n 258: 7. Keep capacity filled with the highest-value ready work.\\r\\n 259: \\r\\n 260: The coordinator must not spend a large initial portion of the task only planning when useful bounded investigation can already proceed safely.\\r\\n 261: \\r\\n 262: ### No unnecessary waiting\\r\\n 263: \\r\\n 264: Unless a real dependency, conflict, safety boundary, exclusive resource, or missing prerequisite requires serialization, do not:\\r\\n 265: \\r\\n 266: - process independent workstreams sequentially;\\r\\n 267: - leave useful worker slots idle while ready work exists;\\r\\n 268: - wait for an entire worker wave when one slot becomes available;\\r\\n 269: - wait for every discovery lane before starting implementation that already has sufficient verified inputs;\\r\\n 270: - wait for every implementation lane before integrating completed independent work when incremental integration is safe;\\r\\n 271: - require every worker to finish before reviewing completed high-priority output;\\r\\n 272: - rerun expensive global checks after every small lane instead of using focused checks and one integrated gate;\\r\\n 273: - make every worker rediscover repository structure;\\r\\n 274: - delay safe read-only fan-out until every planning field is complete;\\r\\n 275: - write oversized plans or status reports whose coordination cost exceeds their practical value;\\r\\n 276: - perform delegable work in the coordinator merely because delegation requires a briefing; or\\r\\n 277: - maintain redundant lanes after sufficient evidence exists.\\r\\n 278: \\r\\n 279: ### Time-aware lane selection\\r\\n 280: \\r\\n 281: In addition to capacity, quality, coverage, verification, and risk value, evaluate each proposed lane by asking:\\r\\n 282: \\r\\n 283: - Will this lane reduce total elapsed completion time?\\r\\n 284: - Does it remove work from the critical path?\\r\\n 285: - Can it execute simultaneously with existing lanes?\\r\\n 286: - Will its expected time savings exceed briefing, monitoring, and integration overhead?\\r\\n 287: - Would combining it with another lane be faster?\\r\\n 288: - Would splitting a slow or broad lane shorten the critical path?\\r\\n 289: - Is the worker capability appropriate for the lane's urgency and complexity?\\r\\n 290: - Can the result be reviewed and integrated promptly?\\r\\n 291: \\r\\n 292: A lane must not be created merely because parallel capacity exists. It should normally improve speed, capacity, quality, coverage, verification, or risk reduction enough to justify its overhead.\\r\\n 293: \\r\\n 294: ### Worker-slot utilization\\r\\n 295: \\r\\n 296: - Meaningful ready worker capacity should normally remain fully utilized.\\r\\n 297: - For substantial tasks, maintain a queue of ready and next-ready lanes ordered by critical-path impact, value, risk, and dependency readiness.\\r\\n 298: - Fill each newly available slot immediately with the highest-value ready lane.\\r\\n 299: - Intentionally leave capacity unused only when no useful independent work exists or when conflict, dependency, safety, ownership, or resource constraints prevent more work.\\r\\n 300: - If substantial ready work exists but available capacity is intentionally unused, briefly record the concrete reason.\\r\\n 301: \\r\\n 302: Do not require twelve workers when fewer meaningful lanes exist. Do require the full useful lane count when enough real, non-overlapping work exists.\\r\\n 303: \\r\\n 304: ## Aggressive concurrency requirement\\r\\n 305: \\r\\n 306: Use the maximum safe and useful concurrency supported by the real dependency graph, available client capacity, file ownership boundaries, runtime constraints, and quality requirements.\\r\\n 307: \\r\\n 308: - **One meaningful lane:** the coordinator may perform it directly. Add a verifier only when it contributes a distinct result. Do not manufacture delegation for a one-line mechanical task.\\r\\n 309: - **Two independent lanes:** start two workers concurrently unless a documented conflict prevents it. Do not finish one before starting the other.\\r\\n 310: - **Three to five independent lanes:** start every ready lane concurrently whenever capacity allows. Do not hold ready work merely to simplify coordination.\\r\\n 311: - **Six to twelve independent lanes:** use the full safe ready-lane count immediately when installed capacity permits. Large audits, investigations, cross-system reviews, multi-surface implementations, testing initiatives, and architecture analyses should normally use six to twelve direct workers when they naturally decompose that far. Twelve direct workers are explicitly acceptable, not required.\\r\\n 312: - **More than twelve independent lanes:** prioritize by dependency, criticality, uncertainty, and value; run waves; and fill each newly available slot immediately rather than waiting for a whole wave to finish.\\r\\n 313: \\r\\n 314: Every worker must own a distinct subsystem, package, route group, component family, animation system, evidence source, bug hypothesis, acceptance criterion, test shard, review concern, risk category, document, non-overlapping file set, or verification responsibility. Never create duplicate prompts, duplicate full-repository scans without different purposes, microscopic artificial lanes, overlapping writers, or workers spawned only to reach twelve.\\r\\n 315: \\r\\n 316: When a substantial task has materially more ready independent work than active workers, either fill safe capacity or record the concrete reason for under-utilization. \\\"The coordinator can do it,\\\" sequential simplicity, prior low worker count, planning effort, or deferring ready work are not sufficient reasons. Unavailable thread capacity, overlapping writes, strict dependency, an exclusive runtime/database/migration/lockfile/generated artifact/browser state, undefined scope, destructive operations, security/authorization boundaries, or high duplication risk are valid reasons.\\r\\n 317: \\r\\n 318: ## Progressive fan-out\\r\\n 319: \\r\\n 320: Verify the minimum safety baseline first: repository root, applicable instructions, user scope, working-tree state, existing task state, current modifications, exclusive resources, and destructive boundaries. Then launch obvious independent read-only lanes immediately while the coordinator refines the plan. Do not delay safe discovery until every planning table is complete. Do not start write lanes until ownership and integration boundaries are explicit and every assigned path has the required verified existing-file, intentional new-file, or unresolved read-only classification. Add newly discovered ready lanes dynamically, collapse redundant lanes, release unused capacity, and preserve useful partial findings.\\r\\n 321: \\r\\n 322: ## Canonical repository context\\r\\n 323: \\r\\n 324: - The canonical Git repository is `Kgray44/treasurehuntSoT`; resolve its root dynamically with `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`. `main` is the canonical integrated branch. This repository is used across computers, so shared instructions must never assume a drive, username, home directory, parent directory, or checkout location.\\r\\n 325: - Root `AGENTS.md` is the canonical repository instruction file. Root `PLANS.md` is the canonical planning standard mandated by this file. Root `.codex/config.toml` is the trusted project configuration location. `Codex_Governing/Agents.md` and `Codex_Governing/Plans.md` are noncanonical retrieval mirrors and are never independent instruction sources.\\r\\n 326: - The canonical active application is the single Next.js App Router package at the repository root, named `forever-treasure-companion` and presented as the unified Tall Tale platform / Forever Treasure Companion. Do not treat snapshots, compatibility routes, validation copies, or former feature branches as alternate applications.\\r\\n 327: - Primary implementation locations are `src/app`, `src/components`, `src/animation`, `src/domain`, `src/lib`, `src/platform`, `src/server`, `src/tall-tale`, `prisma`, `public`, and `scripts`. Tests live beside source as `*.test.*` and under `tests/e2e`.\\r\\n 328: - `Development_Docs` is the canonical shared location for requirements, history, architecture, decisions, and authored plans. `docs` contains maintained product and engineering documentation. `Codex_Chats` is a conversation archive, not application source.\\r\\n 329: - No separate obsolete application tree is currently verified. Former feature branches are integrated history. The development-only `/dev/animations` route is not an obsolete application.\\r\\n 330: - Generated, local, archived, or excluded locations include `node_modules`, `.next`, `out`, `build`, `coverage`, `.runtime`, `.data`, `backups`, local database files, `test-results`, `playwright-report`, `playwright/.auth`, `artifacts/screenshots`, `artifacts/validation`, local logs, and Codex import/cache/temp content identified by `.gitignore`. const patch = "*** Begin Patch\n*** Delete File: \\\\gwplastics.com\\VT\\Users\\kgray\\My Documents\\treasurehunt\\Forever-Treasure-Lanternwake-Phase-5\\scripts\\validate-animation-assets.ts\n*** Add File: \\\\gwplastics.com\\VT\\Users\\kgray\\My Documents\\treasurehunt\\Forever-Treasure-Lanternwake-Phase-5\\scripts\\validate-animation-assets.ts\n+import { createHash } from \"node:crypto\";\n+import fs from \"node:fs\";\n+import path from \"node:path\";\n+\n+type ManifestAsset = Readonly<{\n+  id: string;\n+  type: \"rive\" | \"lottie\";\n+  status: \"runtime-ready\" | \"blocked_external_asset\" | \"development_only\";\n+  expectedPath: string;\n+  sha256: string | null;\n+  fallbackPath: string;\n+  blockedReason?: string;\n+}>;\n+\n+type AnimationManifest = Readonly<{ schemaVersion: number; assets: readonly ManifestAsset[] }>;\n+\n+const root = process.cwd();\n+const animationRoot = path.join(root, \"public\", \"animations\");\n+const manifestPath = path.join(animationRoot, \"manifest.json\");\n+const errors: string[] = [];\n+const blockers: string[] = [];\n+\n+function localPath(publicPath: string) {\n+  if (!publicPath.startsWith(\"/animations/\")) throw new Error(`asset path must remain local to /animations: ${publicPath}`);\n+  return path.join(root, \"public\", publicPath.slice(1));\n+}\n+\n+function sha256(file: string) {\n+  return createHash(\"sha256\").update(fs.readFileSync(file)).digest(\"hex\");\n+}\n+\n+function loadManifest(): AnimationManifest {\n+  try {\n+    const manifest = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(manifestPath, \"utf8\")) as AnimationManifest;\n+    if (manifest.schemaVersion !== 1 || !Array.isArray(manifest.assets)) {\n+      throw new Error(\"expected schemaVersion 1 with an assets array\");\n+    }\n+    return manifest;\n+  } catch (error) {\n+    errors.push(`manifest.json: ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : \"invalid JSON\"}`);\n+    return { schemaVersion: 1, assets: [] };\n+  }\n+}\n+\n+const manifest = loadManifest();\n+const manifestById = new Map(manifest.assets.map((asset) => [asset.id, asset]));\n+const requiredProductionRive = [\"invitationSeal\", \"journalClasp\", \"voyageCompass\", \"finaleMechanism\"];\n+const requiredLottie = [\"moonlitWaves\", \"rollingFog\", \"inkBloom\"];\n+\n+for (const id of [...requiredProductionRive, ...requiredLottie, \"developmentRating\"]) {\n+  if (!manifestById.has(id)) errors.push(`manifest.json: missing required asset ${id}`);\n+}\n+\n+for (const asset of manifest.assets) {\n+  let sourcePath: string;\n+  let fallbackPath: string;\n+  try {\n+    sourcePath = localPath(asset.expectedPath);\n+    fallbackPath = localPath(asset.fallbackPath);\n+  } catch (error) {\n+    errors.push(`manifest ${asset.id}: ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : \"invalid local path\"}`);\n+    continue;\n+  }\n+  if (!fs.existsSync(fallbackPath)) errors.push(`manifest ${asset.id}: missing fallback ${asset.fallbackPath}`);\n+\n+  if (asset.status === \"blocked_external_asset\") {\n+    if (fs.existsSync(sourcePath)) {\n+      errors.push(`manifest ${asset.id}: blocked asset unexpectedly exists; register and validate it atomically`);\n+    }\n+    if (asset.sha256 !== null) errors.push(`manifest ${asset.id}: blocked asset must not claim a SHA-256`);\n+    if (!asset.blockedReason?.trim()) errors.push(`manifest ${asset.id}: blocked asset needs a specific blocked reason`);\n+    blockers.push(`${asset.id}: ${asset.blockedReason ?? \"missing blocked reason\"}`);\n+    continue;\n+  }\n+\n+  if (!fs.existsSync(sourcePath)) {\n+    errors.push(`manifest ${asset.id}: missing local asset ${asset.expectedPath}`);\n+    continue;\n+  }\n+  const actualHash = sha256(sourcePath);\n+  if (!asset.sha256 || actualHash !== asset.sha256.toLowerCase()) {\n+    errors.push(`manifest ${asset.id}: SHA-256 mismatch`);\n+  }\n+  const bytes = fs.readFileSync(sourcePath);\n+  if (asset.type === \"rive\") {\n+    if (bytes.length < 1_000) errors.push(`${asset.id}: unexpectedly small Rive binary`);\n+    if (bytes.subarray(0, 1).toString() === \"{\") errors.push(`${asset.id}: JSON is not a Rive binary`);\n+  } else {\n+    try {\n+      const data = JSON.parse(bytes.toString(\"utf8\")) as Record<string, unknown>;\n+      for (const key of [\"v\", \"fr\", \"ip\", \"op\", \"w\", \"h\", \"layers\"]) {\n+        if (!(key in data)) errors.push(`${asset.id}: missing ${key}`);\n+      }\n+      if (!Array.isArray(data.layers) || data.layers.length === 0) errors.push(`${asset.id}: layers must be non-empty`);\n+      if (typeof data.op !== \"number\" || typeof data.ip !== \"number\" || data.op <= data.ip) {\n+        errors.push(`${asset.id}: invalid frame range`);\n+      }\n+      if (/https?:\\/\\//i.test(JSON.stringify(data))) errors.push(`${asset.id}: remote runtime assets are forbidden`);\n+    } catch (error) {\n+      errors.push(`${asset.id}: ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : \"invalid Lottie JSON\"}`);\n+    }\n+  }\n+}\n+\n+for (const directory of [\"svg\", \"stills\"]) {\n+  const location = path.join(animationRoot, directory);\n+  if (!fs.existsSync(location)) continue;\n+  for (const file of fs.readdirSync(location).filter((name) => name.endsWith(\".svg\"))) {\n+    const source = fs.readFileSync(path.join(location, file), \"utf8\");\n+    if (!source.includes(\"<svg\") || !source.includes(\"</svg>\")) errors.push(`${directory}/${file}: malformed SVG wrapper`);\n+    if (/https?:\\/\\//i.test(source.replace(\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\", \"\"))) {\n+      errors.push(`${directory}/${file}: remote dependency found`);\n+    }\n+  }\n+}\n+\n+if (errors.length) {\n+  console.error(`Animation asset validation failed:\\n${errors.map((error) => `- ${error}`).join(\"\\n\")}`);\n+  process.exit(1);\n+}\n+\n+if (blockers.length) {\n+  console.error(\n+    `Animation asset validation NO-GO: ${blockers.length} production Rive asset-authoring blocker(s) remain.\\n${blockers\n+      .map((blocker) => `- ${blocker}`)\n+      .join(\"\\n\")}`,\n+  );\n+  process.exit(2);\n+}\n+\n+console.log(\"Animation assets validated: 4 production Rive binaries, 3 Lottie JSON assets, and local SVG fallbacks.\");\n*** End Patch";
const r = await tools.apply_patch(patch);
text(typeof r === 'string' ? r : JSON.stringify(r));
